items.title: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
items.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

items.title: Man arrested after three injured in stabbing at Swiss train station
items.summary: The suspect, a 31-year-old Swiss man, has been arrested, police say, as areas around the station are cordoned off.

items.title: US justice department launches criminal investigation into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll, reports say
items.summary: The case centres on whether the former columnist lied about the funding she received for her civil lawsuit against the US president.

items.title: Sixteen pupils killed in Kenya school fire
items.summary: Dozens of people were also injured in the fire, the cause of which is not yet known.

items.title: Heavy Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon after large-scale evacuation orders
items.summary: The Israeli military has ordered Lebanese residents to leave some 17% of the country's territory.

items.title: Jill Biden says she thought husband was having a stroke during 2024 debate
items.summary: The former US first lady told CBS News that she was frightened by Joe Biden's performance against Donald Trump.

items.title: Australia charges woman who returned from Syria with joining Islamic State
items.summary: Several groups of women and children who spent years in a Syrian camp have returned in recent months.

items.title: Australia sues US giant 3M over 'forever chemicals' in firefighting foam
items.summary: The A$2bn case, which centres on contamination at defence sites, is the largest ever brought by the government.

items.title: Google worker charged with using internal data to make $1.2m on bets
items.summary: The longtime Google employee was charged in New York for allegedly breaking insider trading laws.

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for 41 months over actor's ketamine death
items.summary: Kenneth Iwamasa, who pleaded guilty last year to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine causing death, said he "will forever regret" his actions.

items.title: Ebola-hit DR Congo faces 'catastrophic collision' of disease and conflict, WHO warns
items.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said fighting in DR Congo was hampering efforts to stop spread

items.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
items.summary: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.

items.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
items.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

items.title: Oil prices jump after US launches new attacks on Iran
items.summary: The strikes come despite a ceasefire between Tehran and Washington as the two countries hold peace talks.

items.title: Woman caught after 30 years on the run is jailed for armed robberies
items.summary: Klette was finally caught in a flat in Berlin in 2024 and put on trial last year.

items.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
items.summary: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

items.title: Hotel that refused to give tourist tap water acted lawfully, Italian court rules
items.summary: The Italian Supreme Court rejected a tourist's claim that her consumer rights were breached when she was only offered €7 bottled mineral water.

items.title: Ghana welcomes Pope's apology over Catholic Church's role in slavery
items.summary: The West African nation was one of the main gateways for the transatlantic slave trade.

items.title: Trump-backed challenger beats veteran US senator in Texas primary
items.summary: Ken Paxton's victory sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November's midterm elections.

items.title: South Korea detains dissident who fled China in rubber boat
items.summary: Dong Guangping, an activist and former police officer, spent hours at sea before reaching Korean waters.

items.title: We analysed thousands of Trump's posts - here's what we found
items.summary: The BBC's Ros Atkins and BBC Verify sifted through thousands of the president's posts.

items.title: How my brother went from liberal Hollywood actor to manosphere 'messiah'
items.summary: The sister of Mexican influencer El Temach tells the BBC she does not recognise him any more.

items.title: Is 'out of control' US tipping culture spreading overseas?
items.summary: With US waiting staff getting cross at receiving less than 20%, tips are also on the rise elsewhere.

items.title: Duterte ICC trial to start on 30 November: What you need to know
items.summary: Duterte is facing charges relating to a long and bloody war on drugs in which thousands were killed.

items.title: Paul McCartney on playing guitar with Paul Mescal: 'He knew it better than I did!'
items.summary: The music legend on delivering "memory songs" for his 20th solo album, The Boys of Dungeon Lane - and why he's intrigued by the new Beatles biopics.

items.title: In court as Matthew Perry's assistant sentenced
items.summary: BBC's Shaimaa Khalil was in the room as Kenneth Iwamansa, who injected Perry with ketamine, was sentenced to 41 months in prison.

items.title: Watch: Moment rescuers find five people trapped in Laos cave
items.summary: Rescuers in Laos have found five villagers alive inside a flooded cave after they were trapped for a week.

items.title: Watch: UFC arena construction begins at White House ahead of fight
items.summary: The massive structure will host a cage fight as part of celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

items.title: Paris 'punishingly hot' as Western Europe hit by heatwave
items.summary: Hugh Schofield reports from Paris as a heat dome settled over Western Europe bringing temperatures above the average for May.
